text: When the user says a UI change is 'not fixed' — especially after a fresh/incognito load — do NOT blame browser cache, service workers, or tell them to go incognito. It is an implementation problem: open the actual rendered UI at the user's real viewport width, measure against the target, and fix it. Verify by looking at the rendered result, not by asserting the deployed bytes match. Repeatedly citing cache when they have already reloaded is wrong and erodes trust.

An undefined CSS custom property in a shorthand invalidates the ENTIRE declaration (renders as if unset). css/tokens.css defines --space-1..6,8,10,12,16,20 but NOT --space-7 or --space-9. .k-epanel used 'padding: var(--space-6) var(--space-7)' -> whole padding dropped to 0, so the entity card had zero inner padding and content sat flush to the border (reported many times as 'writing too close to the boundary'). When a UI element looks like it has no padding/margin, measure the computed value and check every var() resolves to a DEFINED token; never use --space-7/--space-9.
